mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient real economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum during tightening periods yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 250.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 250.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises signals labor market deterioration and underemployment, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or fails to outperform a baseline momentum strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate demand that translates into improved corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production confirm a contraction in real output that reduces corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
